project.name: C4 link-direction suppression fixture
project.description: Purpose-built link-directionality fixture (plan-3). BOTH routers are genuinely dialable — without the feature, the hub's auto-reverse peer would dial c4-dialer's public endpoint and could win the WireGuard endpoint slot (the reverse-peer race that bypasses a relay/accelerator path). The single edge carries endpoint_host + link_direction=forward, so the hub's [Peer] for c4-dialer keeps AllowedIPs but MUST carry no Endpoint line; the kernel run then proves the tunnel still forms from the dialer's inbound handshake alone and routes both ways.

public_endpoints.note: genuinely dialable — WITHOUT link_direction the hub's reverse peer would dial this (the race)

edges.notes: single-linked: only c4-dialer initiates; the hub's reverse Endpoint is suppressed despite c4-dialer being publicly dialable
